channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Politics Friday: Budget begins to take shape as leaders huddle
item.description: Friday at noon, MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst talks with the top budget adviser to the governor: Gov. Erin Campbell, commissioner of Minnesota Management and Budget. Then, a conversation with the new DFL Party Chair Richard Carlbom.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: A Pentagon inspector general announces investigation into Hegseth’s use of Signal
item.description: The announcement comes after lawmakers voiced concern about whether top national security officials discussed classified information on Signal about a military strike against Houthi rebels in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: WATCH: Dem Jasmine Crockett silent as aide attempts to intimidate, block reporter's question about violence
item.description: Democrat Jasmine Crockett watched as an aide got into a Fox reporter’s face to intimidate him from asking if Democrat rhetoric contributed to violence against Tesla owners and dealers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton gears up for potential Senate run after bribery probe dismissed
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton is gearing up for a potential U.S. Senate run while no longer shadowed by a federal corruption investigation that hung over his rising profile in the Republican Party. That durability would be tested against Republican Sen.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump backs Luna push for House parental proxy voting
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) has been scrambling to break an impasse over the push for proxy voting, after Luna joined with more than 200 Democrats and nine Republicans to use a procedure known as a discharge petition to circumvent leadership and force a vote on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump approval slips to lowest point in second term: Survey
item.description: In the latest poll, 37 percent of respondents approved of Trump’s handling of the economy and 30 percent approved of his approach to addressing the high cost of living in the country.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How the White House calculated reciprocal tariffs
item.description: President Trump triumphantly held up a poster board in the Rose Garden on Wednesday showing the reciprocal tariffs he plans to impose on nearly every country in the world.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: After federal firings, officials worry about program that helps many Minnesotans pay for heat
item.description: Minnesota was scheduled to receive an additional $12 million to $13 million in LIHEAP funds to help another 10,000 households. If that payment is delayed, the program could run out of money by mid-April.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: DeSantis announces support as Florida Senate takes up 'chemtrails' bill
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis expressed his support for a bill banning what he called weather modification "nonsense" before it hits the Senate floor on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Top scientists warn that Trump policies are causing a ‘climate of fear’ in research
item.description: Some 2,000 scientists, including dozens of Nobel Prize winners, have signed an open letter warning that the U.S. lead in science is being “decimated” by the Trump administration’s cuts to research.
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman, MPR News

item.title: Results for key races in suburban elections
item.description: Municipal elections were held in the suburbs of Chicago, with key races including the Aurora mayoral race and Evanston mayor race, as well as a new mayor being elected in Dolton.
item.dc:creator: Anna Roberts, WGN 9

item.title: Legal, medical cannabis fix-it legislation in the works at Minnesota Legislature
item.description: Sen. Scott Dibble, DFL-Minneapolis, is sponsoring several of those cleanup bills for both recreational cannabis and changes to medical marijuana rules. He joined MPR News host Cathy Wurzer ahead of committee hearings Thursday to talk about what the proposed alterations entail.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Layoffs begin at U.S. health agencies responsible for research, tracking disease and regulating food
item.description: Employees across the massive U.S. Health and Human Services Department began receiving notices of dismissal Tuesday in an overhaul ultimately expected to lay off up to 10,000 people.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Yale professor leaving US: Trump launching 'brutal attack' on freedom
item.description: A professor at Yale University said Monday he would be leaving his post and the country over the Trump administration’s “brutal attack” on free speech.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Missouri pastor gets deportation notice for sponsored son from Haiti
item.description: "Do we welcome immigrants in, if they come legally, and are pursing the citizenship process? Is that true or is that not?"
item.dc:creator: Hannah King, Fox 5

item.title: Gov. DeSantis proposes $1K rebate checks to cover Florida homeowners' property tax
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis announced a proposal on Monday that would give Florida homeowners a rebate averaging $1,000 for each homesteaded property in the state.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he’s ‘very angry’ and ‘pissed off’ at Putin during NBC News interview
item.description: President Donald Trump said he was “very angry” and “pissed off” when Russian President Vladimir Putin criticized the credibility of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s leadership, adding that the comments were “not going in the right location.
item.dc:creator: Kristin Welker, WFLA, NBC
